name: WN-71 Approach — The Bluff Base
description: The scrubby grass and loose chalk soil at the foot of the hundred-foot bluff, where the draw begins to rise and the German fortifications become visible as concrete grey against the green — the last fifty yards before the enemy's own ground.
atmosphericDetails: The chalk soil crumbles underfoot and leaves white marks on the hands of anyone who crawls — a detail that will make men visible against the green grass above.
The Tobruk pit of WN-71 is visible from here as a circular concrete rim at the bluff's crest, the barrel of the MG-42 traversing slowly like a metronome.
The smell here is different from the beach — grass and wet chalk instead of salt and cordite, a smell that belongs to a different world entirely.
